_comment: Selectors verified from chat.deepseek.com DOM 2026-07-01. Native CSS only, ordered priority arrays. DeepSeek uses div[role='button'] with class-based disabled state (ds-button--disabled), not the HTML disabled attribute. Send/stop is a single primary circle button (div.ds-button--primary.ds-button--circle): during generation it is the enabled STOP action; when generation finishes it reverts to SEND and, with the input now empty, gains ds-button--disabled. So the completion signal 'div.ds-button--primary.ds-button--circle:not(.ds-button--disabled)' is present while generating and disappears when done — this lets waitForResponseCompletion wait through DeepSeek's reasoning-phase pauses instead of prematurely completing via text stabilization (which produced half messages). The stop button has no aria-label; icons differ only by inner SVG. A copy/regenerate toolbar appears under the finished answer as a secondary 'done' cue.
